title: alchemilla flabellata subsp. colorata
intro: Alchemilla flabellata subsp. colorata, also known as the fan-leaved lady's mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its distinctive fan-shaped leaves and vibrant yellow flowers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Alchemilla flabellata subsp. colorata has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it effective in treating skin issues such as eczema, acne, and wounds. The plant's medicinal properties are also said to promote digestive health, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla flabellata subsp. colorata can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, often used to soothe and calm irritated skin and promote overall well-being.
